title: Dual EMA Fractal Breaker
subtitle: EMA14/EMA200 Trend Filter with Fractal Breakout Entry
author: Christian Benjamin (lynnchris)
verdict: LOW-MEDIUM EXTRACT — EMA14/200 cross as trend filter validates our EMA3 crossover use. Fractal support/resistance breakout entry is comparable to our zone interaction. The EMA200 as absolute trend filter (price above = only buy) is a stronger macro filter than we currently use.

core_concept.name: Fractal Breakout + Dual EMA Trend Filter
core_concept.description: Price breaks above the most recent fractal high (resistance) AND both EMA14 > EMA200 AND price > both EMAs = BUY. Break below fractal low AND EMA14 < EMA200 AND price < both EMAs = SELL.
core_concept.ema200_macro_filter: EMA200 acts as absolute trend filter — never buy when price below EMA200, never sell when price above. This prevents counter-trend trades entirely.
core_concept.ema14_momentum: EMA14 crossover of EMA200 confirms momentum shift. Both together create a dual-confirmation trend filter.
core_concept.relevance_to_creviaderiv: Our EMA3 is fast (tracks M1 price action closely). EMA200 on M15 would add a macro filter — only take BUY signals when M15 close > EMA200. Currently we use market_structure (BULLISH/BEARISH) from StructureEngine as our macro filter, which is more sophisticated than EMA200.

buy: price crosses above most recent fractal resistance level
sell: price crosses below most recent fractal support level

keep: EMA200 as absolute macro trend filter (price side = trade direction)
Fractal level breakout as entry trigger pattern
Dual EMA logic: EMA14 > EMA200 = uptrend; price must be above both
discard: EMA14 as momentum indicator — our EMA3 is more responsive
Simple fractal breakout without zone quality context
improve: Replace EMA200 with our market_structure (BULLISH/BEARISH) — already implemented
Fractal breakout → zone touch: more precise than just crossing a level
Add EMA200 as an optional supplemental macro filter on M15

creviaderiv_insights.ema200_macro: EMA200 on M15 = long-term bias. If market_structure=BULLISH but price < EMA200 M15, the structure break is very fresh and risky. Use EMA200 as a 'maturity' filter: prefer trades where market_structure aligns with EMA200 side.
creviaderiv_insights.fractal_breakout_vs_zone: Fractal breakout = price exceeds a prior extreme. Our zone touch = price enters a supply/demand zone. Zone touch is more precise — it has upper and lower boundaries, not just a single level.
